flashcards.front: What is the key characteristic of Insertion Sort's time complexity?
flashcards.back: It is adaptive: O(n) for sorted/nearly-sorted arrays, O(n²) for reverse-sorted arrays, and O(n²) on average.

flashcards.front: State the loop invariant for the outer loop of Insertion Sort.
flashcards.back: At the start of each iteration i, the subarray A[0..i-1] consists of elements originally in A[0..i-1] but now in sorted order.

flashcards.front: Why is Insertion Sort considered stable?
flashcards.back: Insertion Sort preserves the relative order of equal elements since we shift elements strictly greater than the key to the right.

flashcards.front: What is the space complexity of Insertion Sort?
flashcards.back: O(1) - it is an in-place sorting algorithm requiring only a constant amount of extra memory.

flashcards.front: What is the best-case time complexity of Insertion Sort and when does it occur?
flashcards.back: O(n) - occurs when the input array is already sorted in non-decreasing order.

flashcards.front: What is the worst-case time complexity of Insertion Sort and when does it occur?
flashcards.back: O(n²) - occurs when the input array is sorted in reverse non-increasing order.

flashcards.front: How many comparisons does Insertion Sort make in the worst case for n elements?
How many swaps/shifts does Insertion Sort make in the worst case?
What is the average-case time complexity of Insertion Sort?
Why is Insertion Sort efficient for small or nearly-sorted datasets?
flashcards.back: Its inner loop exits early when the correct position is found, giving O(n) for sorted data and near-O(n) for nearly-sorted data.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between Insertion Sort and the binary search used for optimization?
flashcards.back: Binary search can reduce comparisons to O(log n) per element, but shifts remain O(n), so total complexity remains O(n²).

flashcards.front: Why does Insertion Sort perform better than Selection Sort in practice despite same worst-case complexity?
flashcards.back: Insertion Sort has best-case O(n) and performs fewer swaps, while Selection Sort always performs Θ(n²) comparisons regardless of input.
